new_classes.label_en: Other fermented beverage or mixture of fermented beverages (heading 2206)
new_classes.label_de: Anderes gegorenes Getränk oder Mischung gegorener Getränke (Position 2206)
new_classes.definition_en: Fermented beverage other than beer (2203), wine of fresh grapes (2204), vermouth (2205), or spirits/ethyl alcohol (2207/2208), including cider, perry, mead, saké; mixtures of fermented beverages; and mixtures of fermented beverages with non-alcoholic beverages, not elsewhere specified or included, classified under CN heading 2206. Products obtained through fermentation remain classified here provided they retain the character of products of this heading.

A fermentation process that produces a fermented beverage classifiable under CN heading 2206 (other fermented beverages), distinct from the fermentation processes producing beer (2203), wine (2204), or spirits (2208).
A quality borne by a beverage indicating that it is a mixture of two or more fermented beverages, or a mixture of fermented and non-alcoholic beverages, as described under CN heading 2206.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 2206: (1) the product is obtained through fermentation (modelled via FermentationProcess2206 linked via producedBy); (2) it is disjoint from wine/grape-must (2204), vermouth (2205), spirits (2208), and beer (2203, inherited from Beverage hierarchy); (3) it covers both pure fermented beverages and mixtures with other fermented or non-alcoholic beverages (modelled via MixtureOfFermentedBeveragesQuality). Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the residual character-of-heading retention rule (products must 'retain the character of heading 2206 products') is a legal interpretive rule that cannot be fully axiomatised in OWL-DL without a bespoke quality class that would be circular; (b) explicit named examples (cider, perry, mead, saké) are mentioned in the legal text but are covered by the existing TBox class CiderPerryMeadSakFermentedBeverages2206 — FermentedBeverage2206 is introduced as the more specific running-TBox subclass aligned with 220600; (c) disjointness from BeerMalt2203 is implicitly handled through the Beverage hierarchy but not explicitly axiomatised here due to complement restriction limitations over non-property restrictions.
